signals.headline: South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea's annual inflation rose to 2.2% in March 2026 (from 2.0% prior), below forecasts of 2.4% amid Middle East oil shocks | NO | MED confidence**

Inflation data for March, released around early April 2026, showed a slight uptick to 2.2% year-on-year—still near the Bank of Korea's 2% target but pressured by surging import prices and global energy volatility—yet milder than expected.[2][4][5
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: Solana ETF Inflows Continue on April 16 | YES | HIGH**
signals.raw:
Solana ETF Inflows Continue on April 16 | YES | HIGH**  
U.S. spot Solana ETFs recorded $5.04M net inflows on April 16, following $5.36M the prior day, with cumulative net assets at $835M, signaling sustained institutional demand.[4] This confirms growing validation separate from retail speculation, potentially supporting price amid SOL's 6.3% daily gain to $86.55.[2]
matched_markets: Solana Up or Down - April 17, 4:40AM-4:45AM ET

signals.headline: Tether Backs Drift Relaunch with $150M Recovery Plan on April 16 | YES | HIGH**
signals.raw:
Tether Backs Drift Relaunch with $150M Recovery Plan on April 16 | YES | HIGH**  
Tether supported Drift's relaunch after a $285M exploit, expanding USDT's role on Solana and reinforcing network resilience.[4] This development bolsters Solana's infrastructure for institutional finance, aligning with its lead in adjusted stablecoin volume (32.6% vs. Ethereum's 27.8% as of April 12).[3]
matched_markets: Solana Up or Down - April 17, 4:40AM-4:45AM ET
Ethereum Up or Down - April 17, 1:25PM-1:30PM ET
